inputs.lead_in: Without changing their meanings, convert each of the following sentences into a sentence having the form “$P$ if and only if $Q$.”

following_problems.text: For matrix $A$ to be invertible, it is necessary and sufficient that $\det(A) \neq 0$.
If a function has a constant derivative then it is linear, and conversely.
For an occurrence to become an adventure, it is necessary and sufficient for one to recount it. (Jean-Paul Sartre)

outputs.instruction: Without changing their meanings, convert each of the following sentences into a sentence having the form “$P$ if and only if $Q$.”

inputs.lead_in: Write a truth table for the logical statements in problems 1–9:

following_problems.text: Suppose the statement $((P \wedge Q) \vee R) \Rightarrow (R \vee S)$ is false. Find the truth values of $P, Q, R$ and $S$. (This can be done without a truth table.)
Suppose $P$ is false and that the statement $(R \Rightarrow S) \Leftrightarrow (P \wedge Q)$ is true. Find the truth values of $R$ and $S$. (This can be done without a truth table.)

outputs.instruction: Write a truth table for the logical statements.
